name: Route of the Six Quarries — Monsanto Forest Park
name_original: Percurso das Seis Pedreiras — Parque Florestal de Monsanto
region_intro: Hiking around São Domingos De Benfica is primarily defined by its integration with the expansive Parque Florestal de Monsanto, an urban forest offering diverse landscapes and varied terrain. The region features numerous forest paths, dirt tracks, and hilly sections, providing a natural escape within the city. Additionally, the Parque Urbano da Quinta da Granja offers further green spaces for recreation. These areas are characterized by diverse flora, including cork and holm oaks, and provide opportunities for outdoor activities. Best bus station hiking trails around São Domingos De Benfica The most popular bus station hiking route is Route of the Six Quarries — Monsanto Forest Park, a 2.2 miles (3.5 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This route leads through ancient limestone quarries now integrated into the park's landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Six Quarries of Monsanto – Guarita loop from Estádio do Sport Lisboa e Benfica, an easy 4.3 miles (7.0 km) path. This trail navigates through varied forest paths and offers views of the park's diverse flora. Local hikers also love the Six Quarries of Monsanto – Miradouro dos Moinhos do Mocho loop from Estádio do Sport Lisboa e Benfica, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail leading through varied forest terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Hiking around São Domingos De Benfica is defined by urban forest landscapes, varied terrain with both hilly sections and gentler slopes, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate climbs. The routes in São Domingos De Benfica are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,500 reviews. More than 19,000 hikers have used komoot to explore São Domingos De Benfica's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many bus station hiking trails are available in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;18 bus station hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around São Domingos De Benfica, offering a variety of options for car-free adventures. These routes are designed to start directly from public transport stops, making them easily accessible.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails accessible by bus from São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Yes, São Domingos De Benfica offers a good selection of easy hikes accessible by bus. Out of the 18 available routes, &lt;strong&gt;12 are rated as easy&lt;/strong&gt;. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23653771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Golf loop from National Library of Portugal&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 4.9 km long and features minimal elevation changes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on these bus-accessible trails?
A: The trails primarily traverse the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Parque Florestal de Monsanto&lt;/strong&gt;, often called Lisbon's "green lung." You'll find diverse landscapes including lush forest paths, varied terrain with both hilly and gentler slopes, and areas with cork oak and holm oak trees. Some routes also pass through unique features like ancient limestone quarries.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes from bus stops in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Yes, many of the bus-accessible routes are circular, allowing you to return to your starting point without needing additional transport. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23666438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Six Quarries of Monsanto – Guarita loop from Estádio do Sport Lisboa e Benfica&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.9 km circular hike.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Many trails offer glimpses of significant natural and historical features. You can explore the ancient limestone quarries on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380927974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Six Quarries — Monsanto Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;. The Parque Florestal de Monsanto also features a Cypress Forest with wooden sculptures and parts of the 18th-century Aqueducto das Águas Livres. For a tranquil urban green space, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quinta_da_Granja_(Lisboa)" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Urbano da Quinta da Granja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there viewpoints on the bus station hiking trails?
A: Yes, Parque Florestal de Monsanto, where many of these trails are located, is known for its panoramic viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Miradouro Panorâmico de Monsanto&lt;/strong&gt; offers 360-degree views of the green region and the city. Another notable spot is the &lt;strong&gt;Miradouro do Moinho das Três Cruzes&lt;/strong&gt; (also known as Miradouro do Calhau), providing elevated views surrounded by vegetation.

Q: What do other hikers say about the bus-accessible trails in São Domingos De Benfica?
A: The komoot community highly rates the hiking experience in this area, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.54 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 1,500 ratings. Hikers often praise the accessibility of the trails from public transport, the diverse natural beauty of Monsanto Forest Park, and the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options accessible by bus?
A: Yes, with 12 easy routes available, there are several family-friendly options. The varied terrain of Monsanto Forest Park includes gentler slopes and well-maintained paths suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23666438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Six Quarries of Monsanto – Guarita loop&lt;/a&gt; are generally suitable for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on these bus-accessible hiking trails?
A: Parque Florestal de Monsanto, where many of these trails are located, is generally dog-friendly, allowing leashed dogs on its paths. However, it's always recommended to check specific park regulations or signage at the trailhead for any restrictions on certain areas or during particular times.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike near São Domingos De Benfica?
A: Lisbon generally enjoys a mild climate, making hiking pleasant for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) offer comfortable temperatures and lush scenery. Even winter can be suitable, though some paths might be muddy after rain. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable during those months.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging bus-accessible hikes?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, there are options for those seeking a bit more challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32262198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Six Quarries of Monsanto – Miradouro dos Moinhos do Mocho loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.2 km route that includes some elevation gain, offering a more extensive exploration of the park's diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any lakes or gardens near the bus station hiking trails?
A: Yes, while not directly on every trail, several beautiful green spaces and water features are nearby. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3895825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gulbenkian Garden&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5883754" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake of Parque da Paz&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amália Rodrigues Garden Lake&lt;/a&gt;, which can be visited in conjunction with your hike or as separate excursions.
tips: The Monsanto Interpretation Center (CIM) is the ideal gateway to an in-depth exploration of the Monsanto Forest Park, Lisbon's largest green space. This center offers a unique educational and environmental experience, combining nature, culture, and sustainability.

The Trail of the Six Quarries (Trilho das Seis Pedreiras) is one of the most unique routes in the Monsanto Forest Park in Lisbon. This circular hike takes you through ancient limestone quarries that were exploited for centuries and are now part of the park's natural and cultural landscape.
